preamble: The phonetic transformation from the voiceless alveolo-palatal affricate /t͡ɕ/ to the voiceless alveolar affricate /t͡s/ involves a retraction of the place of articulation. This process is a form of depalatalization, where the point of articulation shifts from the alveolo-palatal region, where the blade of the tongue contacts the area between the alveolar ridge and the hard palate, to the more forward alveolar ridge, typically involving the tongue tip or blade against the alveolar ridge.

languageExamples.note: The shift from /t͡ɕ/ to /t͡s/ is noted as part of apicalization in the development of Proto-Ersuic.

sources: Yu, D. (2012). The ProtoSErsuic Syllable Canon. UC Berkeley Linguistics.
